name: Braised Short Ribs with Root Vegetables
method: Preheat oven to 160°C (320°F). Pat short ribs dry and season generously with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown ribs in batches for 3-4 minutes per side, then remove and set aside.
In the same pot, sauté diced onion for 3 minutes until softened. Add tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Pour in red wine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom. Add beef stock, thyme, and bay leaves. Return ribs to pot.
Cover and braise in oven for 2 hours. Add cut carrots, parsnips, and potatoes, then return to oven for 45 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Remove from oven, skim excess fat from surface, and taste for seasoning. Adjust salt and pepper as needed.
Serve ribs and vegetables in bowls with the rich braising liquid spooned over top.
chef_tip: Brown the meat thoroughly for deeper flavor development—this caramelization is crucial for a restaurant-quality braise.

name: Eggplant Parmesan
method: Slice eggplant lengthwise into 5mm slices. Salt both sides and let sit 20 minutes to draw out moisture, then pat dry.
Set up three shallow bowls: one with flour, one with beaten eggs, one with breadcrumbs mixed with grated parmesan. Season each with salt and pepper.
Dredge each eggplant slice in flour, then egg, then breadcrumb mixture. Heat 60ml olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Fry eggplant slices in batches for 2-3 minutes per side until golden. Transfer to paper towels and season lightly with salt.
Preheat oven to 190°C (375°F). Spread thin layer of tomato sauce on bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Layer half the eggplant slices, then half the remaining sauce, then half the mozzarella. Repeat layers, finishing with mozzarella and parmesan.
Bake for 30 minutes until cheese is melted and bubbling. Let rest 5 minutes, garnish with fresh basil, then serve.
chef_tip: Don't skip salting and draining the eggplant—this removes bitter compounds and excess water that would make the dish soggy.

name: Thai Green Curry with Chicken
method: Cut chicken into 2cm cubes and set aside. Cut vegetables into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a large wok or deep skillet over medium heat. Add curry paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly until fragrant.
Pour in half the coconut milk and stir to combine with the paste. Simmer for 2 minutes.
Add chicken cubes and stir to coat with curry mixture. Cook for 5-6 minutes until mostly cooked through.
Add remaining coconut milk, chicken stock, fish sauce, and palm sugar. Add lime leaves and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add eggplant and bell peppers, simmer for 6 minutes. Add snap peas and cook for 2 more minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Taste and adjust seasoning with more fish sauce or lime juice if needed. Garnish with fresh basil and serve with jasmine rice.
chef_tip: Toast the curry paste briefly in oil before adding liquid—this develops the spices and makes the curry taste more vibrant and authentic.

name: Mediterranean Baked Fish with Tomatoes and Olives
method: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Prepare parchment paper on a large baking sheet.
Halve cherry tomatoes and dice red onion and bell pepper. Mince garlic and chop olives. Zest lemon and cut into thin slices.
In a bowl, combine tomatoes, peppers, olives, capers, onion, garlic, oregano, 2 tbsp olive oil, white wine, and half the lemon zest. Season with salt and pepper.
Arrange fish fillets on prepared parchment paper. Season both sides with salt and pepper. Drizzle with remaining 2 tbsp olive oil.
Spoon the tomato-olive mixture over and around the fish fillets. Top with lemon slices.
Fold parchment paper to create a sealed packet (en papillote style) or cover loosely with foil. Bake for 18-22 minutes until fish flakes easily.
Carefully open packets (watch for steam) and transfer fish and vegetables to serving plates. Drizzle with cooking liquid and garnish with fresh oregano.
chef_tip: Cooking fish en papillote (in parchment) keeps it incredibly moist and allows the flavors to infuse—the steam creates a gentle cooking environment.

name: Chicken Tikka Masala with Basmati Rice
method: Cut chicken into 4cm cubes. Mix yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, garam masala, cumin, coriander, chili powder, turmeric, and salt. Marinate chicken for at least 30 minutes.
Cook rice in 450ml water for 15 minutes until fluffy. Set aside.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Working in batches, cook marinated chicken 6-7 minutes per side until golden. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, add remaining 1 tbsp oil. Finely slice onions and cook over medium heat for 8-10 minutes until deeply caramelized, stirring occasionally.
Add ginger-garlic paste to onions, cook 1 minute. Stir in tomato puree and cook for 3 minutes. Add back chicken pieces.
Stir in heavy cream and coconut milk. Reduce heat and simmer for 12-15 minutes until sauce thickens. Add lemon juice and taste for salt.
Serve chicken tikka masala over basmati rice, garnished with fresh cilantro.
chef_tip: Caramelize the onions properly—those golden, sweet onions form the flavor base of the sauce. Don't rush this step as it takes 8-10 minutes.

name: Risotto ai Funghi with Truffle Oil
method: Heat vegetable stock in a separate pot and keep at a gentle simmer throughout cooking.
Slice mushrooms and sauté in olive oil over high heat for 4-5 minutes until golden; set aside and season with salt and pepper.
Dice onion finely and sauté in butter over medium heat for 2-3 minutes until translucent.
Add arborio rice and stir constantly for 1-2 minutes until rice becomes translucent at the edges.

Pour white wine and stir until nearly absorbed, then begin adding hot stock one ladle at a time, stirring frequently and waiting for each addition to be absorbed before adding the next (about 18-20 minutes total).
When rice is creamy but still has a slight firmness to the bite, remove from heat and stir in butter, parmesan, and the cooked mushrooms.
Drizzle with truffle oil and serve immediately in warm bowls.
chef_tip: Keep your stock at a constant gentle simmer and add it at roughly the same temperature as the rice for even, consistent cooking. Cold stock will shock the rice and interrupt the cooking process.

name: Vietnamese Caramelized Pork with Lemongrass
method: Cut pork shoulder into 3cm cubes and pat dry with paper towels.
Mince garlic and shallots; bruise lemongrass stalks by crushing with the back of a knife.
Heat oil in a large heavy pot over high heat and brown pork cubes in batches (3-4 minutes per batch) until caramelized on all sides; set aside.
In the same pot, sauté minced garlic and shallots for 1-2 minutes until fragrant, then add brown sugar and stir until caramelized (about 2 minutes).
Return pork to the pot and add fish sauce, lemongrass, chicken stock, and black pepper; bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 25-30 minutes until pork is very tender, stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat, stir in lime juice, taste and adjust seasoning with salt if needed, and serve over steamed rice or with bread.
chef_tip: The caramelization of the pork and brown sugar creates the signature deep, complex flavor of this dish. Don't rush the browning step—patience here makes a significant difference in the final result.

name: Spanish Seafood Paella
method: Infuse saffron threads in 100ml of warm fish stock and set aside for 10 minutes.
Dice onion and red pepper; mince garlic; clean squid and cut into rings; devein shrimp.
Heat olive oil in a large 35cm paella pan over medium-high heat and sauté onion for 2-3 minutes until softened.
Add garlic, paprika, and squid rings, cooking for 2 minutes; add rice and toast for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Pour white wine and stir until nearly absorbed, then add remaining fish stock in two additions (add first half, stir occasionally for 10 minutes, then add second half with saffron and its liquid).

After 15 minutes total cooking, arrange shrimp, mussels, and red pepper on top of the rice; scatter frozen peas and cook uncovered for 8-10 minutes until shrimp is pink, mussels have opened, and rice is tender.
Discard any unopened mussels, taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper, and serve from the pan garnished with lemon wedges.
chef_tip: The crispy layer of rice at the bottom of the paella (called socarrat) is prized. Before serving, increase heat to medium-high for the last 1-2 minutes—you should hear a gentle crackling sound but not burning.

name: Mexican Chicken Enchiladas Verdes
method: Boil chicken breasts in salted water with half the onion and garlic for 15 minutes until cooked through; shred finely and season with lime juice, salt, and pepper.
Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F) and mix green salsa with sour cream in a bowl to create the sauce; season with salt and pepper.
Warm tortillas briefly over a gas flame or in a dry pan to make them pliable.
Spread a thin layer of green sauce in the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Fill each tortilla with 3-4 tbsp of shredded chicken, roll tightly, and arrange seam-side down in the baking dish.
Pour remaining green sauce evenly over the enchiladas and sprinkle shredded cheddar cheese over the top.
Bake for 25-30 minutes until cheese is melted and bubbly, then garnish with fresh cilantro and sliced jalapeño before serving.
chef_tip: Warming the tortillas before filling prevents them from cracking and breaking. If they seem too dry, lightly brush them with oil or dip briefly in the green sauce before filling.

name: British Beef and Ale Pie with Root Vegetables
method: Cut beef into 3cm cubes and pat dry; cut vegetables into large chunks. Dust beef with flour, salt, and pepper.
Heat butter in a large heavy pot over high heat and brown beef in batches (4-5 minutes per batch) until caramelized; set aside.
In the same pot, sauté pearl onions and mushrooms for 3-4 minutes until lightly colored; stir in tomato paste for 1 minute.
Return beef to pot, pour ale and beef stock, add bay leaves and thyme sprigs, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer covered for 60 minutes.
Add carrots, parsnips, and potatoes; continue simmering covered for 30 minutes until all vegetables and beef are tender; season to taste with salt and pepper.
Transfer beef and vegetables to a 9x13 inch pie dish and cool slightly; remove bay leaves and thyme sprigs.
Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F), arrange thawed puff pastry over the filling, seal edges with a fork, brush with beaten egg, and bake for 25-30 minutes until golden brown.
chef_tip: The long, slow cooking of the beef in ale develops deep, rich flavors. Don't skip the browning step—this caramelization is essential for building the base of delicious gravy and sauce.

name: Japanese Chicken Teriyaki with Steamed Rice
method: Cut chicken breasts into 2cm cubes and season with salt and pepper.
Heat sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and ginger, cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add chicken pieces and cook 6-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until golden on edges.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, mirin, sake, brown sugar, cornstarch, and water.
Pour teriyaki sauce over chicken and simmer 3-4 minutes, stirring gently, until sauce thickens and coats the chicken.
Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced green onions.
Serve over steamed white rice.
chef_tip: Make sure your heat is high enough when searing the chicken to develop a golden crust, which adds depth to the final dish's flavor.

name: Korean Beef Bulgogi with Vegetable Stir-Fry
method: Slice beef sirloin thinly (about 3mm) against the grain and place in a bowl.
Combine soy sauce, sesame oil, brown sugar, minced garlic, grated pear, and ginger in a bowl to make marinade.
Pour marinade over beef and mix well, then marinate for 15 minutes at room temperature.
Heat 1 tbsp sesame oil in a large skillet over high heat and cook beef in batches 2-3 minutes per batch until cooked through, then set aside.
In the same skillet, stir-fry sliced bell pepper, zucchini, and mushrooms for 4-5 minutes until tender-crisp.
Return beef to the skillet and toss everything together for 1 minute.
Serve over steamed rice, garnished with green onions and sesame seeds.
chef_tip: Slice the beef against the grain for maximum tenderness, and don't overcrowd the pan when cooking—this ensures proper browning and prevents steaming.

name: Vietnamese Fresh Spring Rolls with Peanut Dipping Sauce
method: Cook rice vermicelli according to package directions, then drain and rinse with cold water.
Cut cucumber and carrot into thin matchsticks, about 7cm long.
Fill a shallow bowl with warm water. Dip one rice paper wrapper in water for 2-3 seconds until softened and lay on a damp towel.
Layer on the wrapper: 2 lettuce leaves, small handful of noodles, cucumber, carrot, mint, and cilantro leaves.
Fold the bottom edge of the wrapper over the fillings, fold in the sides, then roll tightly to seal.
For the dipping sauce, whisk together peanut butter, hoisin sauce, lime juice, minced garlic, and water until smooth. Add chili flakes to taste.
Repeat with remaining ingredients to make 12 rolls.
Serve immediately with the peanut dipping sauce.
chef_tip: Keep your workspace organized with all ingredients prepped and in small bowls before rolling—this makes the assembly process faster and prevents the wrappers from drying out.

name: Chinese Sweet and Sour Pork with Bell Peppers
method: Cut pork shoulder into 2cm cubes. Mix cornstarch with egg white and coat pork pieces evenly.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in a large wok or skillet over high heat and cook pork in batches 4-5 minutes until golden, then set aside.
Add remaining oil to the wok, then stir-fry minced garlic and ginger for 30 seconds.
Add chopped bell peppers and onion, stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until slightly softened.
In a small bowl, mix ketchup, rice vinegar, sugar, soy sauce, and chicken broth together.
Return pork to the wok, add pineapple chunks, and pour in the sauce. Toss everything together and simmer for 3-4 minutes until sauce thickens.
Taste and adjust seasonings as needed.
Serve over fluffy white rice.
chef_tip: The key to authentic sweet and sour pork is balancing acidity and sweetness—taste as you go and adjust the vinegar or sugar to achieve the perfect flavor balance for your preference.

name: Thai Green Curry with Vegetables and Coconut Broth
method: Cut tofu into 1.5cm cubes and pat dry with paper towels. Cut bell pepper and zucchini into bite-sized pieces.
Heat a large pot or wok over medium heat and add the curry paste, stirring constantly for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Pour in half the coconut milk and stir well to combine with the curry paste, cooking for 1 minute.
Add remaining coconut milk and vegetable broth, then bring to a gentle simmer.
Add tofu cubes, bell pepper, zucchini, and bamboo shoots. Simmer for 8-10 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Stir in lime juice, fish sauce, and palm sugar. Add minced garlic, ginger, and sliced red chili.
Taste and adjust seasonings as needed, adding more fish sauce for savory or lime juice for brightness.
Serve hot over jasmine rice, garnished with fresh Thai basil leaves.
chef_tip: Bloom the curry paste in oil or coconut milk before adding other liquids—this technique releases the aromatic compounds and creates a more flavorful, cohesive sauce.

name: Butter Chicken with Basmati Rice
method: Cook basmati rice according to package directions and set aside.
Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces and season with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a large pan over medium-high heat and sear chicken until golden on all sides, about 6-8 minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, melt butter and sauté diced onions until softened, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and ginger, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in tomato puree, garam masala, cumin, and paprika. Simmer for 3 minutes to blend flavors.
Return chicken to the pan and add heavy cream. Simmer gently for 15-20 minutes until sauce thickens and chicken is cooked through.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
Serve butter chicken over fluffy basmati rice, garnishing with fresh cilantro if desired.
chef_tip: For a richer sauce, don't skip searing the chicken first—it creates a flavorful fond in the pan that adds depth to your sauce.

name: Lamb Rogan Josh with Pilaf
method: Cut lamb into 3cm cubes and season with salt and pepper. Set aside.
Heat oil in a large, heavy-bottomed pot over medium-high heat. Working in batches, brown the lamb on all sides until deeply colored, about 12 minutes total. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, add cumin seeds and let them sizzle for 30 seconds. Add diced onions and cook until golden, about 8 minutes. Add garlic and ginger, cooking for 2 minutes.
Stir in coriander, garam masala, turmeric, and red chili powder. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant, then add yogurt and stir well.
Add diced tomatoes and return lamb to the pot. Add enough water to cover the lamb halfway. Add cardamom, cinnamon, and bay leaves. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low.
Cover and cook for 50-60 minutes, stirring occasionally, until lamb is tender. The sauce should be thick and rich.
While the lamb cooks, prepare pilaf rice: toast cumin seeds briefly in oil, add rice and stir for 2 minutes, then cook with twice the volume of water until fluffy.
Serve rogan josh over pilaf rice, adjusting salt to taste.
chef_tip: Browning the meat properly is crucial—don't crowd the pan and let each piece develop a golden crust before moving it. This adds tremendous flavor to your final dish.

name: Prawn Masala with Coconut Rice
method: Cook basmati rice in a mixture of water and 100ml coconut milk until fluffy, about 15 minutes. Fluff with a fork and set aside.
Clean and devein prawns, then pat dry with paper towels. Set aside.
Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat. Sauté diced onions until golden, about 5 minutes. Add garlic, ginger, and green chilies, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add cumin, coriander, turmeric, and red chili powder. Cook for 30 seconds, then add diced tomatoes. Simmer for 5 minutes until tomatoes break down into a paste-like consistency.
Pour in 200ml coconut milk and bring to a gentle simmer. Add salt to taste.
Add prawns to the pan and cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they turn pink and are cooked through. Do not overcook or they'll become tough.
Finish with lime juice and fresh cilantro.
Serve prawn masala over coconut rice, spooning sauce generously over top.
chef_tip: Pat the prawns dry before cooking—excess moisture prevents them from developing a proper sear and can make the sauce watery. Add them to the pan only when the sauce is simmering gently.

name: Saag Paneer with Naan Bread
method: Cut paneer into 2cm cubes. Heat oil in a pan and shallow-fry paneer cubes until golden on all sides, about 5 minutes. Drain on paper towels and set aside.
Blanch spinach in boiling salted water for 2 minutes, then immediately shock in ice water. Drain well and roughly chop.
Warm naan bread in a dry pan or over an open flame until puffed, about 30 seconds per side. Keep warm.

In a large pan, melt butter and add cumin seeds. Let them sizzle for 30 seconds. Add finely diced onions and cook until golden, about 6 minutes. Add garlic, ginger, and green chilies, cooking for 1 minute.
Add turmeric and garam masala, cooking for 30 seconds until fragrant. Stir in the chopped spinach and mix well.
Add yogurt and cream, stirring to combine. Simmer gently for 5 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly. Add salt and pepper to taste.
Gently fold in the fried paneer cubes, being careful not to break them. Simmer for 2-3 minutes until paneer is heated through.
Serve saag paneer in bowls with warm naan bread on the side for dipping into the creamy spinach sauce.
chef_tip: Frying the paneer first gives it a slight crust that helps it hold its shape when added to the sauce. If your spinach is watery after blanching, squeeze it gently in a clean kitchen towel to remove excess moisture.

name: Chicken Biryani with Raita
method: Marinate chicken thighs in yogurt with minced garlic, ginger, green chili, turmeric, coriander, and salt for at least 30 minutes.

Soak rice in water for 20 minutes. In a large pot, bring water to a boil with whole spices (cardamom, cinnamon, cloves, bay leaves, cumin). Add rice and cook until 70% done, about 7-8 minutes. Drain immediately.
Heat 3 tbsp ghee in a heavy-bottomed pot over medium-high heat. Fry sliced onions until deep golden brown and crispy, about 10 minutes. Set half aside for garnish.
Spread remaining fried onions on the bottom of the pot. Layer half the marinated chicken over the onions, then sprinkle with cilantro and mint.

Layer the partially cooked rice over the chicken. Add remaining chicken and top with final layer of rice. Drizzle remaining 2 tbsp ghee over the top. Sprinkle with reserved crispy onions, cilantro, and mint.
Cover the pot tightly with aluminum foil, then place the lid on top. Cook on medium heat for 5 minutes until steam forms, then reduce heat to low and cook for 35-40 minutes undisturbed.
While biryani cooks, prepare raita: mix yogurt with grated cucumber, salt, and a pinch of cumin.
When cooking time is complete, turn off heat and let biryani rest for 5 minutes without opening. Gently fluff with a fork, mixing the layers. Serve with cool raita on the side.
chef_tip: The key to perfect biryani is layering and undisturbed cooking. Resist the urge to open the lid during cooking—the steam trapped inside cooks the rice perfectly. If your pot isn't heavy-bottomed, use a tawa (griddle) or baking sheet under it to prevent burning.

name: Garlic Butter Prawns with Crusty Bread
method: Peel and devein prawns, leaving tails intact. Pat dry with paper towels.
Mince garlic finely and chop parsley. Zest and juice the lemon.
Heat 30g butter in a large pan over high heat until foaming. Season prawns with salt and pepper.
Add prawns to the pan and sear 2 minutes per side until pink. Remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium, add remaining butter and garlic to the pan. Cook 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Pour in white wine and lemon juice, scraping up any brown bits. Return prawns to pan and toss well.
Cook 2 more minutes until sauce thickens slightly. Stir in lemon zest and fresh parsley.
Serve immediately with crusty bread to soak up the buttery sauce.
chef_tip: Dry prawns before cooking ensures they sear properly instead of steaming. Don't overcook or they'll become tough.

name: Herb-Crusted Baked Salmon with Roasted Vegetables
method: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Chop dill and thyme finely. Mix with lemon zest, breadcrumbs, 2 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Place salmon skin-side down on the baking sheet. Spread mustard on top of each fillet.
Press the herb mixture onto the mustard-coated salmon, gently pressing so it adheres.
Cut zucchini into rounds and halve cherry tomatoes. Toss with minced garlic, remaining olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Arrange vegetables around the salmon on the baking sheet.
Bake 18-22 minutes until salmon flakes easily and vegetables are tender.
Serve hot with a wedge of fresh lemon.
chef_tip: Place vegetables around the fish rather than on top so they roast perfectly while the salmon cooks gently.

name: Crispy Fish and Chips with Tartare Sauce
method: Cut potatoes into 8mm thick chips. Soak in cold water for 30 minutes, then drain and pat dry thoroughly.
Heat oil to 130°C (265°F). Fry chips in batches for 8-10 minutes until soft but not colored. Remove and drain.
For tartare sauce: finely chop capers and cornichons, mix with mayonnaise and lemon juice. Season to taste.
Increase oil temperature to 190°C (375°F). Pat fish dry and season with salt and pepper.
Mix flour, cornstarch, baking powder, salt, and pepper. Whisk in cold beer until smooth batter forms.
Dip fish fillets in batter, coating completely. Fry 6-8 minutes until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels.
Increase oil temperature to 160°C (320°F). Give chips a final fry for 2-3 minutes until golden and crispy.
Serve fish and chips hot with tartare sauce and lemon wedges.
chef_tip: The double-frying method for chips creates a fluffy inside and crispy outside. The first fry cooks them, the second crisps them.

name: Seafood Linguine with White Wine Sauce
method: Cook linguine according to package directions until al dente. Reserve 200ml pasta water before draining.
Mince garlic and finely chop shallots. Peel prawns and devein. Clean mussels and clams, discarding any that won't close.
Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add shallots and garlic, cook 2 minutes until softened.
Increase heat to high. Add prawns and cook 1-2 minutes per side, then remove and set aside.
Pour in white wine and fish stock, scraping up brown bits. Add mussels and clams, cover, and steam 4-5 minutes.
Discard any shellfish that haven't opened. Return prawns to pan. Add cooked pasta and toss well.
Add pasta water as needed for a silky sauce. Season with salt, pepper, and chili flakes. Finish with lemon juice.
Garnish generously with fresh parsley and serve immediately with extra lemon wedges.
chef_tip: Add shellfish just before pasta to prevent overcooking. The residual heat will continue cooking them while the sauce comes together.

name: Pan-Seared Cod with Caper-Lemon Brown Butter and Polenta
method: Bring stock to a boil in a saucepan. Slowly whisk in polenta, stirring constantly to avoid lumps.
Reduce heat to low and cook 15-20 minutes, stirring frequently, until polenta is creamy and thick.
Stir in 30g butter and grated parmesan. Season with salt and pepper. Cover and keep warm.
Pat cod fillets dry and season generously with salt and pepper. Zest both lemons and juice one.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Place cod skin-side up in the pan. Sear 4-5 minutes without moving until golden, then carefully flip.
Cook 3-4 more minutes until fish is cooked through. Remove cod and set aside.
In the same pan, reduce heat to medium. Add remaining butter and sage, cook until butter turns golden brown (2-3 minutes).
Add capers, lemon juice, and zest to the brown butter sauce. Spoon over cod and serve with creamy polenta.
chef_tip: Browning butter transforms simple fish into something luxurious. Watch carefully as the butter solids turn golden—this happens fast and burnt butter tastes bitter.

name: Carne Asada Beef Tacos with Cilantro-Lime Rice
method: Mince garlic and combine with lime juice, orange juice, cumin, chili powder, and 2 tbsp olive oil to create marinade.
Cut beef into 2cm-thick steaks and coat thoroughly with marinade. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Heat remaining olive oil in a large skillet over high heat until smoking.
Remove beef from marinade and sear for 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare. Rest 5 minutes then slice into strips.
Cook rice according to package directions, then fluff and mix with fresh cilantro juice and lime zest.
Warm tortillas in a dry skillet or over an open flame for 20 seconds per side.
Assemble tacos by filling tortillas with beef, diced onion, and fresh cilantro.
Serve with cilantro-lime rice and lime wedges on the side.
chef_tip: Don't skip marinating the beef—the citrus acid tenderizes the meat while the spices infuse deep flavor. Slice against the grain for maximum tenderness.

name: Slow-Cooked Pork Carnitas with Black Beans and Corn
method: Cut pork shoulder into 5cm cubes and season generously with salt and pepper.
Peel and crush garlic, slice onion, and juice oranges and limes into a slow cooker.
Add pork to slow cooker with orange juice, lime juice, garlic, cumin, oregano, and bay leaves.
Cook on low setting for 8 hours or high setting for 4 hours until pork is extremely tender and shreds easily.
Remove bay leaves and drain excess liquid, reserving 120ml for sauce.
Warm black beans and corn in a saucepan with reserved liquid and a pinch of cumin for 5-7 minutes.
Shred pork with two forks and crisp up portions in a hot skillet for 3-4 minutes if desired.
Serve pork topped with fresh cilantro over black beans and corn mixture with lime wedges.
chef_tip: For extra crispy edges, spread shredded carnitas on a baking tray and broil for 2-3 minutes before serving. The contrast between tender inside and crispy outside is spectacular.

name: Spiced Black Bean and Sweet Potato Chili
method: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat and sauté diced onion and minced garlic for 5 minutes until softened.
Cut sweet potatoes into 1cm cubes and add to pot along with tomato paste, stirring to coat for 2 minutes.
Pour in canned tomatoes, black beans, and vegetable broth, then add cumin, chili powder, smoked paprika, and cayenne.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 30-35 minutes until sweet potato is very tender.
Taste and adjust seasonings with salt, pepper, and lime juice.
Simmer for additional 5 minutes to let flavors meld together.
Ladle into bowls and garnish with fresh cilantro.
chef_tip: Make a big batch and freeze portions in containers—this chili actually tastes better the next day as flavors continue to develop. Reheat gently on the stovetop.

name: Chicken Burrito Bowls with Cilantro-Lime Cauliflower Rice
method: Cut chicken breasts into 2cm pieces and season with chili powder, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and cook chicken pieces for 8-10 minutes until golden and cooked through. Remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, add remaining olive oil and sauté diced red bell pepper and red onion for 4 minutes.
Add cauliflower rice and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Season with lime juice, salt, and pepper. Fold in cilantro.
Warm black beans and corn together in a small saucepan, then drain excess liquid.
Mix Greek yogurt with lime juice and minced garlic to create a cilantro-lime crema.
Assemble bowls by layering cauliflower rice as base, then top with chicken, black beans, corn, and diced red onion.
Drizzle with cilantro-lime crema and salsa, garnish with fresh cilantro and lime wedge.
chef_tip: Prep your vegetables and cook the cauliflower rice ahead of time for a quick weeknight dinner. You can add the warm chicken right before serving to keep everything fresh.

name: Chile Relleno Casserole with Crema and Queso
method: Roast poblano peppers directly over a gas flame or under the broiler until blackened all over, about 8 minutes. Place in a plastic bag for 5 minutes to steam, then peel off skin carefully. Make a slit and remove seeds.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a saucepan over medium heat and sauté diced onion and minced garlic for 5 minutes until golden.
Add tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, then pour in canned tomatoes, cumin, oregano, and vegetable broth. Simmer for 10 minutes, then blend until smooth or leave chunky as preferred.
Whisk eggs with Mexican crema, salt, and pepper to create a custard mixture.
Stuff each poblano pepper with 45g of oaxaca cheese and arrange in a greased 9x13 inch baking dish.
Pour tomato sauce over peppers, then pour egg-crema custard over top, allowing it to flow around the peppers.
Bake at 180°C (350°F) for 25-30 minutes until custard is set but still slightly jiggly in the center.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and remaining shredded cheese, then return to oven for 2 minutes until cheese melts slightly.
chef_tip: Don't over-bake the custard—it should be creamy and slightly custardy in the center, not completely firm. The carry-over heat will continue cooking it after removal from the oven.

name: Greek Eggplant Moussaka with Béchamel Topping
method: Slice eggplants lengthwise into 8mm strips, brush with olive oil, and roast at 200°C for 20 minutes until soft. Set aside.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large pan, sauté diced onion and garlic until fragrant, then add ground lamb and cook 8-10 minutes until browned.
Add tomato sauce, red wine, cinnamon, oregano, salt and pepper to the meat mixture. Simmer for 15 minutes until sauce thickens.
Make béchamel sauce: melt butter in a saucepan, whisk in flour to create a roux, gradually add milk while stirring constantly, cook 8-10 minutes until thick and smooth. Stir in 50g parmesan.
Layer half the eggplant slices in a buttered 9x13 inch baking dish, spread half the meat sauce over top, then remaining eggplant and remaining meat sauce.
Pour béchamel sauce evenly over the top layer, sprinkle remaining 30g parmesan cheese.
Bake at 180°C for 35-40 minutes until golden brown and bubbling at edges.
Rest 10 minutes before serving to allow layers to set.
chef_tip: Pre-roasting the eggplant removes excess moisture and prevents a soggy moussaka—this step makes all the difference in texture and flavor absorption.

name: Moroccan Spiced Chicken Tagine with Apricots and Almonds
method: Season chicken thighs with salt and pepper. Heat olive oil in a large tagine or heavy-bottomed pot over medium-high heat.
Brown chicken pieces 3-4 minutes per side, then remove and set aside on a plate.
In the same pot, sauté sliced onions, minced garlic, and ginger for 5 minutes until soft. Stir in cumin, paprika, cinnamon, and turmeric, cooking 1 minute until fragrant.
Add tomato paste and stir to combine, then return chicken to pot. Pour in chicken stock, nestle apricots and almonds around the meat.
Bring to a simmer, cover and reduce heat to low. Cook for 35-40 minutes until chicken is very tender.
Uncover and simmer final 5 minutes to reduce sauce slightly if needed.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve with couscous or crusty bread.
chef_tip: Cooking with the lid on until near the end creates steam that tenderizes the chicken while the apricots naturally thicken and slightly sweeten the sauce—no need for added thickeners.

name: Shakshuka with Spiced Tomato Sauce and Crispy Bread
method: Heat olive oil in a large, shallow pan over medium heat. Dice onion and bell pepper, mince garlic, then sauté until softened, about 7 minutes.
Stir in cumin, paprika, and cayenne pepper, cooking 1 minute until fragrant.
Add crushed tomatoes with their juices, season with salt and pepper. Simmer for 10 minutes uncovered until sauce thickens slightly.
Make 6 small wells in the sauce with the back of a spoon, spacing them evenly.
Crack an egg into each well, being careful not to break the yolks.
Cover the pan and cook on medium heat for 6-8 minutes until egg whites are set but yolks remain runny.
Crumble feta cheese over top and sprinkle with fresh chopped parsley.
Serve immediately with thick slices of toasted crusty bread for dipping in both eggs and sauce.
chef_tip: Keep the pan covered during the final cooking stage to gently cook the eggs through steam—this ensures creamy runny yolks while the whites set perfectly, rather than frying them in oil which can toughen the whites.

name: Lamb Kofta with Herbed Yogurt Sauce and Tabbouleh
method: Finely chop onion, garlic, mint, and parsley. Mix ground lamb with these aromatics, cumin, paprika, cinnamon, egg, breadcrumbs, salt and pepper until just combined.
Form mixture into 12 oval kofta shapes around wooden skewers that have been soaked in water for 30 minutes.
Prepare tabbouleh: soak bulghur wheat in hot water for 15 minutes, then drain well. Combine diced cucumber, halved cherry tomatoes, remaining fresh parsley, and mint.
Mix tabbouleh with 3 tbsp olive oil and 2 tbsp lemon juice, season with salt and pepper.
Make yogurt sauce: stir together Greek yogurt, 1 tbsp lemon juice, minced dill, salt and pepper.
Heat grill pan or barbecue to high heat, lightly oil the grates.
Grill kofta skewers 12-15 minutes total, turning every 3 minutes until cooked through and browned on all sides.
Serve kofta hot with herbed yogurt sauce and chilled tabbouleh salad on the side.
chef_tip: Don't overwork the lamb mixture when combining—handle it gently to keep the kofta tender and moist. Overworking develops the gluten in breadcrumbs and makes them dense and tough.

name: Stuffed Bell Peppers with Feta, Rice, and Pine Nuts
method: Cut tops off bell peppers and remove seeds and membranes. Lightly brush outsides with olive oil, place in a baking dish with 100ml water, and roast at 180°C for 15 minutes.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large pan, sauté diced onion and garlic until fragrant, then add rice and toast for 2 minutes.
Add vegetable stock, bring to boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until rice is tender and stock is absorbed.
Stir in fresh spinach until wilted, then fold in crumbled feta, toasted pine nuts, chopped sun-dried tomatoes, sliced black olives, oregano, dill, and lemon zest.
Season filling with salt and pepper to taste.
Carefully spoon rice mixture into the partially cooked bell peppers, packing gently.
Return stuffed peppers to oven and bake for 20-25 minutes at 180°C until peppers are tender and filling is heated through.
Serve warm, drizzling any pan juices over the top.
chef_tip: Pre-roasting the peppers for 15 minutes ensures they become tender while keeping their shape—this prevents them from collapsing during the final bake while the filling heats through.

name: Grilled Ribeye Steak with Chimichurri and Roasted Root Vegetables
method: Cut potatoes and carrots into 2cm chunks, toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast at 200°C (400°F) for 25 minutes until golden.
While vegetables roast, prepare chimichurri: finely chop parsley, cilantro, and garlic. Mix in a bowl with 2 tbsp olive oil, red wine vinegar, salt, chili flakes, and black pepper.
Remove steak from refrigerator 15 minutes before grilling. Pat dry with paper towels and season generously with salt and pepper on both sides.
Preheat grill to high heat (200°C or hotter). Grill steak 4-5 minutes per side for medium-rare, pressing gently with tongs once during cooking.
Transfer steak to a cutting board and rest 5 minutes. Spoon half the chimichurri over the steak.
Plate roasted vegetables alongside the steak and drizzle remaining chimichurri over vegetables. Serve immediately.
chef_tip: Let your steak rest at room temperature for 15 minutes before grilling—this ensures even cooking throughout the meat and a better crust formation.

name: Thai-Style Grilled Chicken with Green Mango Salad
method: Pound garlic and Thai chilies together in a mortar, then mix with coconut milk, 2 tbsp lime juice, and fish sauce. Rub marinade over chicken breasts and refrigerate 30 minutes.
Peel and julienne green mango into thin matchsticks. Halve cherry tomatoes and thinly slice red onion. Combine in a bowl with remaining lime juice, remaining fish sauce (1 tbsp), and a pinch of salt.
Preheat grill to medium-high heat (180°C). Oil grill grates with vegetable oil using a brush.
Grill marinated chicken 6-7 minutes per side until cooked through and charred at edges, turning once halfway through.
Remove chicken from grill and rest 3 minutes, then slice into 1cm strips.
Add fresh mint and cilantro to mango salad and toss gently. Top with grilled chicken slices and scatter roasted peanuts over the top.
chef_tip: Don't skip the pounding step—bruising the garlic and chilies releases their oils and flavors, creating a more aromatic and flavorful marinade.

name: BBQ Spiced Pork Ribs with Crispy Slaw and Cornbread
method: Mix brown sugar, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, black pepper, cayenne, and salt in a small bowl. Pat ribs dry and massage dry rub into both sides, then wrap in foil and refrigerate 2 hours.
Preheat oven to 130°C (260°F). Place foil-wrapped ribs on a baking sheet and bake for 2.5 hours until meat is tender and pulls away from bones.
While ribs cook, finely shred cabbage and julienne carrots. Whisk together apple cider vinegar, mayonnaise, and honey. Toss with cabbage and carrots, season with salt and pepper, and refrigerate.
In the last 20 minutes of cooking, remove ribs from foil, brush with BBQ sauce, and place directly on oven racks at 200°C (400°F) to caramelize the sauce.
Heat cornbread muffins according to package directions, about 10 minutes at 180°C (350°F).
Cut ribs between bones and pile onto a plate. Serve with a generous portion of crispy slaw, warm cornbread, and extra BBQ sauce on the side.
chef_tip: The low-temperature oven cooking makes ribs incredibly tender—the foil traps steam which breaks down connective tissue. Don't skip this step for quick-grilled results.

name: Mediterranean Herb-Marinated Grilled Lamb Chops with Lemon Potatoes
method: Combine 4 tbsp olive oil with minced garlic, chopped rosemary, thyme, and oregano in a bowl. Add 2 tbsp lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Coat lamb chops thoroughly and marinate 1 hour.

Cut potatoes into 3cm cubes. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large pan, add potatoes, and sauté 5 minutes until edges begin to brown. Pour in chicken stock, lemon juice, and lemon zest. Cover and simmer 15-20 minutes until fork-tender.
Preheat grill to high heat (200°C). Remove lamb chops from marinade and allow excess to drip off.
Grill lamb chops 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare, pressing gently with tongs occasionally. Chops should have a charred crust and pink center.
Transfer chops to a warm plate and rest 3 minutes. Taste potatoes and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
Plate potatoes in the center, arrange lamb chops alongside, crumble feta cheese over potatoes, and garnish with fresh parsley.
chef_tip: Marinating lamb in acidic lemon juice and herbs for at least 1 hour tenderizes the meat and infuses it with flavor—this is especially important for lamb chops which can be tough if undermarinated.

name: Honey-Dijon Glazed Grilled Salmon with Asparagus and Quinoa
method: Rinse quinoa under cold water. In a saucepan, bring vegetable stock to boil, add quinoa, reduce heat, cover, and simmer 15 minutes until liquid is absorbed. Fluff with a fork and set aside.
Whisk together honey, Dijon mustard, balsamic vinegar, minced garlic, and fresh thyme in a small bowl to create the glaze. Set aside 2 tbsp for basting.
Toss asparagus with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper. Preheat grill to medium-high heat (180°C) and grill asparagus 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, until lightly charred. Transfer to a plate.
Pat salmon dry and brush skin-side with 1 tbsp olive oil. Season flesh-side with salt and pepper. Place skin-side down on grill grates and cook 5 minutes without moving.
Carefully flip salmon, brush with honey-mustard glaze, and cook 4-5 minutes more until cooked through. Brush with remaining glaze in the last minute.
Plate quinoa, arrange grilled asparagus alongside, top with glazed salmon fillet, squeeze fresh lemon juice over the fish, and sprinkle sesame seeds on top.
chef_tip: Grilling salmon skin-side down first creates a protective barrier that prevents the delicate flesh from sticking to the grates and breaking apart when flipped.

name: Creamy Truffle Mushroom Tagliatelle
method: Bring a large pot of salted water to boil and cook tagliatelle according to package directions until al dente.
While pasta cooks, slice mushrooms and mince garlic. Heat butter in a large pan over medium-high heat.
Add mushrooms and sauté for 5-6 minutes until golden and liquid releases, then stir in garlic and thyme and cook 1 minute.
Pour in heavy cream and bring to a gentle simmer, cooking 3-4 minutes until slightly thickened.
Drain pasta and toss into the mushroom sauce, stirring gently to coat. Remove from heat.
Stir in grated parmesan and truffle oil, season with salt and pepper to taste.
Divide into bowls and garnish with extra parmesan and fresh thyme.
chef_tip: Slice mushrooms uniformly and don't crowd the pan—this ensures even browning and helps develop deep umami flavors instead of steaming them.

name: Spiced Prawn Linguine with Garlic and Chilli
method: Bring salted water to boil and cook linguine until al dente, approximately 10-12 minutes.
While pasta cooks, slice garlic thinly and finely chop chilli. Peel and devein prawns if necessary.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat, add garlic and chilli, and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Increase heat to medium-high, add prawns and cook 2-3 minutes per side until pink and opaque.
Deglaze pan with white wine, scraping the bottom, and simmer for 2 minutes to reduce slightly.
Drain pasta and add to the prawn skillet, tossing gently to combine. Season with salt and pepper.
Finish with fresh lemon juice and chopped parsley, toss once more and serve immediately.
chef_tip: Don't overcook the prawns—they continue cooking briefly after removing from heat, so stop when just opaque to avoid a rubbery texture.

name: Crispy Sausage and Roasted Fennel Rigatoni
method: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Remove sausages from casings and break into chunks. Slice fennel into wedges and dice onion.
Toss fennel with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes until caramelized.
Meanwhile, heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Add sausage chunks and cook 6-7 minutes until browned, breaking apart as it cooks.
Add diced onion and minced garlic, cooking for 3 minutes until softened. Stir in fennel seeds and oregano, cook 1 minute.
Pour in tinned tomatoes and bring to a simmer, cooking for 10 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
Bring a pot of salted water to boil and cook rigatoni until al dente. Drain and add to the sausage sauce.
Fold in roasted fennel gently, top with grated parmesan, and serve.
chef_tip: Don't skip roasting the fennel separately—this caramelizes the natural sugars and mellows the strong anise flavor, making it sweeter and more palatable.

name: Indian-Spiced Chickpea and Spinach Pasta
method: Bring salted water to boil and cook penne until al dente. Drain and set aside.
Heat vegetable oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them crackle for 30 seconds.
Dice onion finely, mince garlic, and grate ginger. Add to the pan and cook 4-5 minutes until onion softens.
Stir in ground coriander, garam masala, and turmeric, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add tinned tomatoes and chickpeas (drained and rinsed), simmer for 8 minutes.
Pour in coconut milk and add fresh spinach, stirring until wilted (about 2-3 minutes). Season with salt and lemon juice.
Gently fold in cooked penne and serve hot with extra cilantro if desired.
chef_tip: Toasting the cumin seeds in hot oil before adding other ingredients blooms their essential oils, creating much deeper spice flavor than using ground cumin alone.

name: Baked Mac and Cheese with Smoked Bacon Crust
method: Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Cook macaroni in salted boiling water until just al dente (about 6 minutes), then drain.
Chop bacon and cook in a skillet over medium-high heat until crispy (about 5-6 minutes). Remove with a slotted spoon, leaving 1 tbsp fat in the pan.
Add butter to the pan with minced onion and cook 2 minutes. Whisk in flour to create a roux, cooking for 1 minute while stirring constantly.
Gradually whisk in warm milk, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Cook for 4-5 minutes until sauce thickens and coats the back of a spoon.
Remove from heat and stir in shredded cheddar, gruyere, mustard, salt, pepper, and cayenne until cheese melts completely.
Fold in cooked macaroni and crispy bacon, then transfer to a buttered 2-liter baking dish. Mix breadcrumbs with grated parmesan and scatter over the top.
Bake for 20-25 minutes until the top is golden brown and the sauce bubbles around the edges. Rest 5 minutes before serving.
chef_tip: Make the cheese sauce with warm milk rather than cold to prevent lumps, and add cheese off the heat to prevent stringiness and ensure a silky, creamy texture.

name: Vietnamese Caramelised Pork and Broccoli Stir-Fry
method: Slice pork thinly against the grain into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat until smoking.
Add garlic and ginger, stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add pork and cook 4-5 minutes, stirring constantly, until golden and cooked through.
Add broccoli florets and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until tender-crisp.
Mix in fish sauce and palm sugar, tossing for 1 minute until caramelised.
Finish with lime juice and top with sliced chilli and sesame seeds.
Serve immediately over jasmine rice.
chef_tip: Slice the pork very thinly and have all ingredients prepped before you start cooking—stir-fries cook so fast that preparation is key to success.

name: Spanish Chorizo and Chickpea One-Pan Dinner
method: Cut chorizo into thick rounds and slice onion and pepper into chunks.
Heat olive oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat and cook chorizo 3-4 minutes until browned, then remove.
In the same pan, add onion, pepper, and garlic, sautéing for 3 minutes until softened.
Stir in paprika and cumin, cooking for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add canned tomatoes, chickpeas, broth, and return chorizo to the pan.
Simmer for 12-15 minutes until vegetables are tender and flavours meld.
Stir in fresh spinach and cook 2 minutes until wilted.
Season with salt, pepper, and lemon juice before serving.
chef_tip: Use a large, heavy-bottomed saucepan so everything cooks evenly and you have room to stir without spilling over the sides.

name: Teriyaki Salmon with Sesame Bok Choy and Ginger Rice
method: Mix soy sauce, mirin, rice vinegar, and 1 tbsp ginger in a bowl for teriyaki sauce.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over high heat.
Season salmon with salt and pepper, then place skin-side up in the hot skillet for 4 minutes.
Flip salmon and cook another 3-4 minutes until nearly cooked through, then remove to a plate.
In the same skillet, add bok choy and remaining ginger, stir-frying for 3 minutes until tender.
Return salmon to pan, pour teriyaki sauce over top, and cook for 1-2 minutes until sauce thickens and salmon is cooked through.
Toss bok choy with sesame oil and arrange with salmon on a bed of ginger rice.
Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced spring onions.
chef_tip: Don't move the salmon while the skin is cooking—let it sit undisturbed for the first 4 minutes to get that crispy, golden skin.

name: Italian Green Lentil and Roasted Vegetable Pasta
method: Bring vegetable broth to a boil in a saucepan, add lentils and thyme, and simmer for 18-20 minutes until just tender but not mushy.
Meanwhile, toss zucchini, cherry tomatoes, and red onion with 2 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Roast in a 210°C (410°F) oven for 12-15 minutes until caramelised and tender.
Cook pasta according to package directions in salted boiling water, drain and reserve 1 cup pasta water.
Heat remaining olive oil in a large pan over medium heat, add garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Add cooked lentils, roasted vegetables, and balsamic vinegar to the pan, tossing gently.
Add cooked pasta and 100ml pasta water, stirring until silky and combined.
Finish with fresh basil, top with parmesan cheese, and serve immediately.
chef_tip: Green lentils hold their shape better than brown lentils, making this dish more visually appealing—don't overcook them or they'll become mushy.

name: Thai Green Curry Chicken with Jasmine Rice and Cucumber Salad
method: Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces and slice cucumber for salad.
Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat and stir in green curry paste for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Pour in coconut milk gradually, stirring constantly to combine with the paste.
Add chicken pieces and simmer for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until cooked through.
Add green beans, aubergine, and bamboo shoots, cooking for 4-5 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Season with fish sauce, palm sugar, and lime juice, adjusting to taste.
Mix cucumber with sliced chilli and a pinch of salt for a quick salad.
Serve curry over jasmine rice, topped with fresh basil and accompanied by cucumber salad.
chef_tip: Bloom the curry paste in oil before adding the coconut milk—this releases the oils and intensifies the flavour significantly.

name: Crispy Aubergine and Lentil Stacks with Tahini Drizzle
method: Slice aubergine lengthwise into 8mm thick strips. Brush with olive oil on both sides and arrange on baking trays.
Roast at 220°C (425°F) for 20-25 minutes, turning halfway, until golden and tender.
Meanwhile, warm lentils in a pan with minced garlic, cumin, and paprika for 5 minutes over medium heat.
Whisk tahini with lemon juice, 3 tbsp water, minced garlic, salt and pepper to create a pourable sauce.
Layer roasted aubergine slices alternately with the spiced lentil mixture on warm plates.
Drizzle tahini sauce generously over each stack.
Finish with fresh parsley and pomegranate seeds.
Serve immediately while aubergine is still warm.
chef_tip: Salt the aubergine slices 15 minutes before cooking and pat dry—this removes excess moisture and helps them crisp up beautifully.

name: Korean-Style Tofu and Vegetable Bibimbap
method: Cook rice according to package instructions and set aside.
Cut tofu into 1cm cubes, pat dry, and pan-fry in vegetable oil over high heat until golden on all sides (8 minutes). Toss with 1 tbsp soy sauce.
Blanch spinach for 2 minutes, drain, squeeze dry, and mix with sesame oil and minced garlic.
Julienne carrots and sauté in a wok for 3 minutes. In the same wok, cook sliced mushrooms until tender.
Lightly blanch bean sprouts for 1 minute and drain well.
Mix gochujang, remaining sesame oil, rice vinegar, and 2 tbsp water to create the sauce.
Arrange rice in a hot stone bowl (or regular bowl), top with vegetable components and fried tofu in separate sections.
Fry an egg sunny-side up and place on top. Drizzle with gochujang sauce, sprinkle sesame seeds, and serve with the hot bowl sizzling.
chef_tip: Press the tofu between clean towels with a weight on top for 30 minutes before cooking to extract moisture and achieve better browning.

name: Peruvian Causa Limeña with Avocado and Crab
method: Boil peeled and chopped yellow potatoes in salted water until very tender (15 minutes). Drain and mash thoroughly.
Mix mashed potatoes with ají amarillo paste, 3 tbsp olive oil, 2 tbsp lime juice, salt, and white pepper until smooth and bright yellow.
Combine crab meat with diced celery, thinly sliced red onion, cilantro, remaining lime juice, 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Spread half the potato mixture on a serving platter, creating a thin even layer.
Layer sliced avocado over the potato, then top with the crab mixture.
Cover with remaining potato mixture, smoothing the top.
Hard-boil eggs for 8 minutes, cool, peel, and slice.
Decorate the causa with egg slices and black olives. Serve chilled or at room temperature.
chef_tip: Use fresh yellow potatoes (like Peruvian varieties) rather than white—they have superior flavor and texture for this dish, and create that distinctive golden color.

name: Herbed Goat Cheese and Walnut Tart with Roasted Grapes
method: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Place puff pastry on a lined baking tray and prick with a fork.
Brush pastry with beaten egg yolk and bake for 15 minutes until lightly golden.
Meanwhile, toss grapes with 1 tbsp olive oil, balsamic vinegar, honey, thyme, and salt. Spread on a separate tray and roast for 12-15 minutes.
Crumble walnuts roughly and toast in a dry pan for 3 minutes until fragrant. Mix with minced garlic, rosemary, remaining olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Spread the walnut mixture evenly over the partially baked pastry.
Crumble goat cheese over the walnuts and return to oven for 8-10 minutes until cheese softens slightly.
Remove from oven and top with roasted grapes and their pan juices.
Rest for 5 minutes, drizzle with any remaining honey, and serve warm.
chef_tip: Toast the walnuts separately before adding to the tart—this intensifies their flavor and prevents them from becoming soggy from the cheese.

name: Thai Red Curry Paste Snapper with Bamboo Shoots and Basil
method: Cook jasmine rice according to package instructions and set aside.
Heat oil in a large pan over medium-high heat and fry minced garlic and sliced shallot until fragrant (1 minute).
Add red curry paste and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly to release the flavors.
Pour in coconut milk slowly, stirring well to incorporate the curry paste evenly.
Add bamboo shoots, lime leaves, palm sugar, and fish sauce. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Gently place snapper fillets into the simmering curry and cook for 8-10 minutes until the fish is just cooked through (flesh flakes easily with a fork).
Remove from heat, stir in lime juice, and scatter Thai basil and sliced red chillies over the top.
Serve in bowls over jasmine rice, ensuring each portion has plenty of curry sauce.
chef_tip: Add the snapper gently and avoid stirring once it's in the pan—fish breaks apart easily, so minimal handling keeps fillets intact and beautiful.

name: Classic Homemade Beef Burgers with Caramelised Onions
method: Slice onion thinly and cook in butter over medium heat for 12-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until golden and caramelised. Season with salt and set aside.
Combine ground beef, bread crumbs, minced garlic, egg, salt and pepper in a bowl. Mix gently with hands until just combined—do not overwork.
Form mixture into 4 equal patties about 1.5cm thick. Make a small indent with your thumb in the centre of each patty.
Heat a skillet or grill to medium-high heat. Cook patties 3-4 minutes per side for medium doneness, placing cheese on top during the last minute of cooking.
Toast burger buns lightly in the same skillet if desired.
Assemble burgers: place patty on bun, add caramelised onions, tomato slice, and lettuce. Serve immediately.
chef_tip: The indent in the centre of each patty prevents the burger from puffing up while cooking and ensures even thickness throughout.

name: Crispy Chicken Schnitzel with Lemon and Fresh Herbs
method: Place chicken breasts between cling film and pound gently with a meat mallet until 0.5cm thick, being careful not to tear the meat.
Set up three shallow bowls with flour, beaten eggs, and panko crumbs mixed with salt and pepper respectively.
Coat each chicken piece in flour, shaking off excess, then dip in egg, and finally coat thoroughly with panko mixture. Place on a plate.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until a bread crumb sizzles immediately when dropped in.
Carefully place schnitzel in hot oil and fry 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through. Transfer to paper towels to drain.
Squeeze fresh lemon juice over the schnitzel, garnish with fresh parsley and thyme, and serve immediately with lemon wedges.
chef_tip: Pound the chicken evenly to ensure uniform cooking throughout, and keep oil at the right temperature—too cool and it becomes greasy, too hot and the crumbs burn before the chicken cooks.

name: Creamy Seafood and Potato Pie with Herbed Crust
method: Peel and cube potatoes, boil in salted water for 8 minutes until partially cooked, then drain and set aside. Dice onion finely.
Melt 40g butter in a large pan, sauté onion for 3 minutes until soft. Sprinkle flour and stir constantly for 1 minute to create a roux.
Gradually whisk in milk and fish stock, stirring continuously until smooth and thickened (about 3-4 minutes). Season with salt and pepper.
Cut fish into 3cm chunks, add to the sauce along with prawns, potatoes, peas and fresh dill. Simmer gently for 5 minutes until seafood is just cooked. Pour mixture into a buttered pie dish.
Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Place pastry sheet over filling, trim edges, and crimp the border with a fork. Brush with beaten egg yolk.
Bake for 25-30 minutes until pastry is golden brown and filling is bubbling at the edges. Rest for 5 minutes before serving.
chef_tip: Partially cooking the potatoes beforehand ensures they finish at the same time as the sauce and seafood, preventing either from being overcooked.

name: Slow Cooker Pulled Pork with Smoky BBQ Sauce and Coleslaw
method: Slice onions and mince garlic. Season pork shoulder all over with salt, pepper and smoked paprika, rubbing it in well.
Place onion and garlic in slow cooker base, then place pork on top. Mix BBQ sauce, apple cider vinegar and brown sugar together, then pour over pork.
Cover and cook on low for 7-8 hours (or high for 4-5 hours) until pork is extremely tender and shreds easily with a fork.
Remove pork to a cutting board and shred using two forks, discarding any excess fat. Return shredded pork to slow cooker and mix through the sauce. Keep warm on low setting.
Make coleslaw: combine coleslaw mix, mayonnaise, white vinegar and a pinch of salt. Mix well and refrigerate until serving.
Toast buns lightly, then fill generously with pulled pork. Top with a spoonful of fresh coleslaw and serve immediately.
chef_tip: For extra flavour, let the cooked pulled pork sit in the sauce on the warm slow cooker setting for at least 30 minutes before serving, allowing the meat to absorb the smoky sauce flavours.

name: Creamy Chicken and Leek Pie with Wholemeal Crust
method: Make pastry: mix wholemeal flour and 0.5 tsp salt, rub in cold butter until breadcrumb texture, add ice water gradually until dough forms. Chill for 30 minutes.
Poach chicken in simmering stock for 12 minutes until cooked, remove and shred. Slice leeks into 1cm rounds, quarter mushrooms.
Melt 80g butter in a large pan, sauté leeks and mushrooms for 5 minutes. Sprinkle flour and stir for 1 minute, then gradually whisk in milk and remaining stock until thickened.
Add shredded chicken, fresh thyme, salt and pepper to the sauce. Simmer for 3 minutes, then transfer to a pie dish and cool slightly.
Preheat oven to 190°C (375°F). Roll out pastry to fit dish, place over filling, trim edges and crimp. Brush with beaten egg yolk and make a small steam vent.
Bake for 30-35 minutes until pastry is golden brown and filling is bubbling slightly at the edges. Rest 5 minutes before serving.
chef_tip: Keep your butter and water ice cold when making the pastry—this ensures a crisp, flaky crust by preventing the gluten from developing too much during mixing.

name: Classic French Onion Soup with Gruyère Croutons
method: Slice onions thinly and melt butter in a large pot over medium heat.
Add onions and cook for 25-30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until deeply caramelised and golden brown.
Deglaze the pot with white wine, scraping up any browned bits, and simmer for 2 minutes.
Pour in beef stock, add thyme, and simmer for 10 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
Toast baguette slices until crispy and top each with grated gruyère.
Ladle soup into bowls and top with cheese croutons, broiling under high heat for 2-3 minutes until cheese melts and bubbles.
Serve immediately while the cheese is molten.
chef_tip: The key to authentic French onion soup is patience with caramelising the onions—don't rush this step as it develops the deep, complex flavour.

name: Spiced Carrot and Ginger Bisque with Coconut Cream
method: Heat olive oil in a large pot and toast cumin seeds for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add diced onion and cook for 4 minutes until softened, then add grated ginger and turmeric, stirring for 1 minute.
Add chopped carrots and vegetable stock, bringing to a boil, then simmer for 20 minutes until carrots are tender.
Blend soup until completely smooth using an immersion blender or transfer to a food processor in batches.
Stir in coconut cream and lime juice, heating gently without boiling for 2-3 minutes.
Season with salt and chilli flakes to taste.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with toasted pumpkin seeds and a dollop of yoghurt.
chef_tip: Ginger releases more flavour when grated rather than minced, and adding it before the carrots allows it to infuse the oil.

name: Hearty Tuscan White Bean and Kale Soup
method: Heat olive oil in a large pot and sauté minced garlic, diced carrots, and celery for 6 minutes until softened.
Add chopped kale (without stems) and cook for 3 minutes until wilted slightly.
Pour in vegetable stock and canned tomatoes with their juice, add rosemary sprigs and chilli flakes.
Bring to a simmer and cook for 15 minutes to develop flavours.
Drain and rinse canned beans, then add to the pot and simmer for 10 more minutes.
Remove rosemary sprigs and season generously with salt and black pepper.
Serve in bowls and drizzle with quality olive oil.
chef_tip: Remove the thick central stem from kale leaves before chopping to avoid tough, stringy pieces in your soup.

name: Vietnamese Pork and Shrimp Wonton Noodle Soup
method: Mince shrimp and combine with ground pork, minced garlic, ginger, and 1 sliced spring onion. Season with 0.5 tbsp fish sauce and sesame oil.
Place 1 tsp of filling in the centre of each wonton wrapper, wet edges with water, fold into triangle, then bring corners together.
Bring chicken stock to a simmer in a large pot with remaining fish sauce and ginger slices.
Cook wontons in boiling salted water for 5-6 minutes until they float and cooked through, then transfer to a colander.
Cook egg noodles according to package directions, drain and divide among bowls.
Add pak choi to simmering stock and cook for 2 minutes until just tender.
Pour hot stock and vegetables over noodles, top with wontons, and garnish with sliced spring onions, coriander, and a lime wedge.
chef_tip: Chill the pork and shrimp filling before assembling wontons—cold filling is easier to work with and prevents wrappers from tearing.

name: Spanish Chorizo and Red Lentil Soup with Smoked Paprika
method: Slice chorizo into half-moons and fry in a large pot over medium-high heat for 4 minutes until lightly browned. Remove with a slotted spoon.
In the same pot with chorizo oils, sauté diced onion and bell pepper for 5 minutes until softened, add minced garlic and paprika, cooking for 1 minute.
Add red lentils, chicken stock, canned tomatoes with their juice, and bay leaf.
Return chorizo to the pot, bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 20-25 minutes until lentils are tender and beginning to break down.
Remove bay leaf and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Ladle into bowls and garnish with fresh parsley and a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil.
Serve with crusty bread for mopping up the broth.
chef_tip: Don't stir red lentil soup too much once they're cooking, as they break down and make the soup creamy naturally without the need for blending.

name: Grilled Chicken Caesar Salad with Garlic Croutons
method: Pat chicken dry and season generously with salt and pepper. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a grill pan over high heat.
Grill chicken 6-7 minutes per side until cooked through and golden. Rest 5 minutes, then slice into strips.
Cut bread into cubes, toss with minced garlic and 1 tbsp olive oil. Toast in a 200°C oven for 8 minutes until golden.
Whisk together lemon juice, anchovy paste, mustard, egg yolk, and remaining 1 tbsp olive oil to make dressing.
Tear romaine lettuce and place in a large bowl. Pour dressing over and toss gently.
Top salad with grilled chicken slices, croutons, and shaved parmesan cheese.
Serve immediately with extra lemon wedges on the side.
chef_tip: Slice the chicken against the grain to ensure it's tender and easier to eat in a salad.

name: Mediterranean Feta and Olive Salad with Grilled Vegetables
method: Slice zucchini, eggplant, and red pepper lengthwise into 5mm strips. Slice onion into rings.
Brush vegetables with 2 tbsp olive oil, season with salt and pepper. Grill on high heat 3-4 minutes per side until charred.
Chop grilled vegetables into bite-sized pieces and place in a large bowl with mixed leaves and halved tomatoes.
Whisk together red wine vinegar, minced garlic, oregano, and remaining 2 tbsp olive oil.
Pour dressing over salad and toss gently. Top with crumbled feta cheese and kalamata olives.
Serve at room temperature for best flavor.
chef_tip: Prepare grilled vegetables in advance and store them at room temperature—they actually taste better when not chilled.

name: Japanese Tuna Niçoise Salad with Wasabi Dressing
method: Boil new potatoes until tender (12-15 minutes), then halve them. Blanch green beans for 3 minutes until tender-crisp.
Boil quail eggs for 3 minutes until soft-boiled. Cool and peel gently.
Pat tuna dry, season with salt and pepper. Sear in a hot pan for 30 seconds per side to create a crust while keeping interior rare.
Whisk together sesame oil, rice vinegar, wasabi paste, soy sauce, and minced ginger to make dressing.
Arrange salad leaves on a platter. Arrange potatoes, green beans, tomatoes, cucumber, and eggs around the center.
Slice tuna against the grain and place on top. Drizzle with wasabi dressing and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Serve immediately while tuna is still warm.
chef_tip: Don't overcook the tuna—sear it quickly on high heat to get a golden crust while keeping the inside rare and tender.

name: Spiced Chickpea and Quinoa Power Bowl with Tahini Dressing
method: Rinse quinoa and cook according to package directions (about 15 minutes). Fluff with a fork and season lightly.
Cut sweet potato into 1cm cubes, toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, cumin, paprika, and salt. Roast at 200°C for 18 minutes until golden.
Massage kale with a pinch of salt to soften. Thinly slice red cabbage, dice cucumber, and halve tomatoes.
Drain and rinse canned chickpeas. Toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, cayenne pepper, and salt. Roast with sweet potato for the last 10 minutes.
Whisk together tahini, lemon juice, minced garlic, remaining 1 tbsp olive oil, and 3 tbsp water until smooth and creamy.
Divide quinoa among bowls. Top with kale, roasted vegetables and chickpeas, red cabbage, cucumber, and tomatoes.
Drizzle with tahini dressing and sprinkle pomegranate seeds on top.
Serve at room temperature or warm.
chef_tip: Make the tahini dressing ahead of time—it actually tastes better when flavors have time to meld, and it keeps for up to 5 days refrigerated.

name: Vietnamese-Inspired Cold Noodle Salad with Grilled Shrimp
method: Bring a pot of water to boil and cook rice vermicelli according to package directions (usually 4-5 minutes). Drain and rinse with cold water.
Pat shrimp dry, season with salt and pepper. Heat a grill pan or skillet over high heat and grill shrimp 2 minutes per side until pink.
Whisk together lime juice, fish sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, minced garlic, minced red chili, and palm sugar to make dressing.
Cut cucumber lengthwise and slice thinly. Julienne carrot and slice red pepper into thin strips.
Divide cooked noodles among serving bowls. Top with grilled shrimp and arrange vegetables in sections around the bowl.
Pour dressing evenly over each bowl and toss gently to combine.
Garnish generously with fresh mint, cilantro, basil, and crushed peanuts. Serve immediately.
chef_tip: Prepare all vegetables ahead of time and store separately, but dress the noodles just before serving to keep them from becoming soggy.

name: Classic BLT with Crispy Bacon and Tomato Aioli
method: Cook bacon in a skillet over medium heat for 6-8 minutes until crispy, turning occasionally.
Transfer bacon to paper towels to drain and cool.
Mix mayonnaise with minced garlic and lemon juice in a small bowl to make garlic aioli.
Lightly toast bread slices until golden brown.
Spread garlic aioli on both bread slices.
Layer lettuce, tomato slices, and crispy bacon on one slice.
Top with second bread slice and cut diagonally.
Serve immediately while bacon is still warm.
chef_tip: Pat the tomato slices dry with a paper towel before assembling to prevent the bread from becoming soggy.

name: Italian Tuna Melt with Fresh Mozzarella and Basil
method: Drain tuna and mix with finely diced red onion, celery, mayonnaise, lemon juice, and capers.
Season tuna mixture with salt and pepper to taste.
Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).
Slice mozzarella into thin pieces and tear basil leaves.
Brush ciabatta slices with olive oil on both sides.
Spread tuna mixture evenly on one bread slice.
Top with mozzarella slices and basil leaves, then cover with second bread slice.
Wrap in foil and bake for 8-10 minutes until bread is crispy and cheese is melted.
chef_tip: Use good quality canned tuna packed in olive oil for better flavor and texture in your melt.

name: Croque Monsieur with Béchamel and Gruyère
method: Make béchamel sauce: melt 1.5 tbsp butter in a small saucepan, add flour and whisk for 1 minute.
Gradually add milk while whisking until smooth, then cook 3-4 minutes until thickened, season with nutmeg, salt, and pepper.
Stir in dijon mustard and let cool slightly.
Spread thin layer of béchamel on both bread slices.
Layer ham and half the gruyère on one slice, top with second slice.
Butter the outside of the sandwich with remaining 1.5 tbsp butter.
Heat a skillet over medium heat and place sandwich in it.
Cook 4-5 minutes until golden, carefully flip, top with remaining gruyère, and cook another 4-5 minutes until cheese melts and bread is crispy.
chef_tip: Use a spatula and another utensil to help flip the sandwich so it doesn't fall apart—keep firm pressure during the flip.

name: Avocado and Egg Toast with Crispy Sourdough and Dukkah
method: Toast sourdough slices until crispy and golden brown on both sides.
Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat and crack both eggs into the pan.
Cook eggs until whites are set but yolks remain runny, about 4-5 minutes, season with salt and pepper.
Halve avocado, remove pit, and scoop flesh into a bowl.
Mash avocado with lemon juice, pinch of sea salt, and pepper.
Spread mashed avocado evenly on both toast slices.
Top each slice with a fried egg.
Sprinkle dukkah over eggs, garnish with thin slices of red chilli, and drizzle lightly with olive oil.
chef_tip: Choose avocados that yield slightly to pressure but aren't mushy—they should mash smoothly without being overripe.

name: Crispy Club Sandwich with Turkey, Bacon, and Pesto Aioli
method: Cook bacon in a skillet over medium heat for 6-8 minutes until crispy, transfer to paper towels to drain.
Toast all three bread slices until golden and crispy.
Mix mayonnaise with fresh basil pesto in a small bowl.
Spread pesto aioli on all three toast slices.
Layer turkey, bacon, lettuce, and tomato on first slice.
Top with second slice and layer remaining ingredients on it.
Finish with third slice pesto-side down.
Cut diagonally into quarters and secure with toothpicks, serve immediately.
chef_tip: Toast the bread immediately before assembling to keep it crispy—if it cools down, the sandwich will be soggy despite the aioli barrier.

name: Spiced Chicken Shawarma Wrap with Tahini Sauce
method: Mince garlic cloves and combine with yogurt, cumin, paprika, lemon juice, salt and pepper to create marinade.
Cut chicken breast into thin strips and coat thoroughly with marinade. Let sit for 15 minutes.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add marinated chicken strips and cook for 12-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until cooked through and edges are caramelised.
Warm tortillas in a dry pan for 30 seconds on each side.
Mix tahini with 2 tablespoons water and a squeeze of lemon juice to create sauce consistency.
Assemble wraps by spreading tahini sauce on each tortilla, adding chicken, tomatoes, red onion and fresh parsley.
Roll tightly, wrap in foil and serve immediately.
chef_tip: Marinating the chicken in yogurt tenderises the meat while the spices infuse deeply. Don't skip this step even if you're short on time.

name: Crispy Falafel and Hummus Pitta with Roasted Vegetables
method: Drain and rinse canned chickpeas thoroughly, pat dry with paper towels.
Pulse chickpeas, diced onion, minced garlic, cilantro, cumin and coriander in a food processor until coarse texture forms.
Add flour, salt and pepper, pulse until mixture just comes together. Don't overprocess or falafel will be dense.
Heat vegetable oil in a deep pan to 180°C (350°F). Form falafel mixture into 12-14 balls using wet hands.
Fry falafel in batches for 3-4 minutes until deep golden brown, turning halfway. Drain on paper towels.
Warm pitta breads in a dry pan or toaster for 1-2 minutes.
Slice pittas open and spread hummus and tahini inside, then fill with falafel, cucumber, tomatoes and salad leaves.
Serve immediately while falafel is still warm and crispy.
chef_tip: Keep your falafel mixture cold before frying and don't skip the drying step for chickpeas—moisture is the enemy of crispiness.

name: Smoked Salmon and Cream Cheese Bagel with Capers and Red Onion
method: Slice bagels in half and toast in a toaster or toaster oven for 3-4 minutes until golden and crispy.
Remove from heat and immediately spread 60g cream cheese on each warm bagel half while it softens slightly.
Thinly slice red onion and separate into rings.
Layer smoked salmon generously over the cream cheese on each bagel half.
Top with capers, red onion rings, fresh dill fronds and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice.
Season lightly with salt and black pepper.
Serve immediately on a board or plate.
chef_tip: Toast the bagels right before assembling—the warmth helps the cream cheese spread smoothly and creates the perfect texture contrast with the cold salmon.

name: Grilled Vegetable and Halloumi Flatbread with Basil Oil
method: Cut zucchini, bell peppers and eggplant into long strips approximately 0.5cm thick, toss with 2 tablespoons olive oil, salt and pepper.
Heat a grill pan or barbecue to high heat and grill vegetables for 3-4 minutes per side until charred and tender, set aside.
Slice halloumi cheese into 0.5cm thick slices and grill for 1-2 minutes per side until golden marks appear, don't flip multiple times.
Create basil oil by blending fresh basil, minced garlic, 3 tablespoons olive oil and balsamic vinegar until smooth.
Lightly brush flatbreads with 0.5 tablespoon olive oil and warm in the grill pan for 1 minute on each side until soft and pliable.
Lay flatbreads on a board and brush with basil oil mixture.
Layer grilled vegetables, halloumi slices and halved cherry tomatoes on each flatbread.
Fold or cut as desired and serve warm, with extra basil oil drizzled on top.
chef_tip: Make the basil oil in advance and store in the fridge—it intensifies in flavour and lets you quickly assemble an impressive flatbread any day of the week.

name: Turkey and Avocado Wrap with Chipotle Mayo and Black Beans
method: Pulse mayonnaise with chipotle peppers and 0.5 tablespoon lime juice in a food processor until smooth. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Drain and rinse black beans, then warm in a small pan with 0.5 teaspoon cumin over medium heat for 3 minutes.
Warm flour tortillas in a dry pan for 30 seconds on each side until pliable.
Dice avocado and toss gently with remaining lime juice, salt and pepper to prevent browning.
Dice red bell pepper and red onion into small pieces.
Warm turkey slices gently in a dry pan or microwave for 1-2 minutes just before assembly.
Lay tortillas flat and spread 1.5 tablespoons chipotle mayo on each, leaving a border.
Layer turkey, warm black beans, avocado, corn, bell pepper, red onion and fresh cilantro on each tortilla, then roll tightly, tucking in sides as you go.
Wrap in foil and serve immediately or chill until needed.
chef_tip: Toss the avocado with lime juice immediately after cutting—this prevents oxidation and keeps it bright green throughout the day if you're packing for lunch.

name: Soy-Ginger Chicken Fried Rice with Egg and Vegetables
method: Dice chicken breast into small 1cm cubes and season with salt and pepper.
Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over high heat. Stir-fry chicken until cooked through (5-6 minutes), then remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil to the wok, pour in beaten eggs and scramble until just cooked, then remove.
Add minced ginger and garlic to the wok, cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add frozen vegetables and stir-fry for 2 minutes, then add the cold rice, breaking up any clumps.
